steps.title: Identify the parallel structure
steps.content: The sentence contains a list of exemplars from three fields. Each item in the list should begin with a location marker—in astronomy, in chemistry, in mathematics—to maintain consistent parallel structure.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces a list. Option A uses abound: with in astronomy, as the start of the first list item, maintaining parallel structure where all items begin with a location marker.

Option B places in astronomy in the main clause, leaving the first list item (there is the Cassegrain reflector) without the location marker that the other items have, breaking parallelism.

Options C and D use incorrect punctuation for introducing or separating list items.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
